title: IsScrollChainingEnabled ineffective on mobile (ComboBox)
author: dariostrm
body: ### Describe the bug When opening the dropdown of a ComboBox which doesn't react when the user scrolls (either because there is enough space for all items in the dropdown, or because the user already scrolled to the top/bottom and is trying to scroll up/down), the parent ScrollViewer ends up recognizing and handling the scroll gesture on mobile. This results in the Popup still floating in the same absolute position on the screen, while the stuff behind it is moving. This was supposed to be mitigated by `IsScrollChainingEnabled` being set to false and that does mitigate it on desktop, but not on mobile. Maybe it's because the `ScrollGestureEvent` is not being raised in this case on the Popup? ### To Reproduce - New Avalonia Cross-platform project (version 12.1.0) - Slap something like this onto the MainView.axaml: ```xaml ``` - Start the Android app - Click on the first ComboBox and scroll up within the popup of the ComboBox (or scroll all the way down in the popup and then try scrolling further down) - OR click on the second ComboBox and try scrolling up or down with your finger inside the Popup ### Expected behavior Expected behavior would be to just not let the user scroll up/down within the parent ScrollViewer when the dropdown is open because it results in this ugly state where the popup stays floating and becomes "detached" from the ComboBox ### Avalonia version 12.1.0 ### OS Android ### Additional context This is what fixed the issue on desktop, I think: [https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia/pull/20503](https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia/pull/20503) _No response_

title: Native Wayland: Window.Hide() then Window.Show() fails to remap the window after the first hide
author: alper-han
body:
### Describe the bug

On native Avalonia Wayland, a Window that has been shown once cannot be reliably shown again after Window.Hide().

The first Window.Show() works when the application starts hidden and the window has never been shown before. After the window is hidden once, every later Window.Show() call returns without an exception and Window.IsVisible becomes true, but no physical window is displayed by the compositor.

This reproduces on Hyprland. The same application flow works when Avalonia uses X11/XWayland instead of its native Wayland backend.

Video: https://youtu.be/5zqtp34w3Cc

### To Reproduce

1. Run an Avalonia 12.1.0 application using the native Wayland backend on Hyprland.
2. Start with the main Window created but never shown.
3. Call Window.Show(). The window appears correctly.
4. Call Window.Hide().
5. Call Window.Show() again on the same Window instance.

Observed:
- The second Show() returns without an exception.
- Window.IsVisible becomes true.
- The window is not physically displayed.
- A later toggle sees IsVisible == true even though no window is visible.

The issue reproduces with normal, floating, and maximized window states.

For comparison, forcing the same application to use X11/XWayland with:

env XDG_SESSION_TYPE=x11 <application> --start-minimized

does not reproduce the problem.

### Expected behavior

A Window hidden with Window.Hide() should be displayed again by Window.Show() on every hide/show cycle, as it is on X11.

### Avalonia version

12.1.0

### OS

Linux

### Additional context

Environment:
- Desktop environment / compositor: Hyprland
- Session type: Wayland
- WAYLAND_DISPLAY: wayland-1
- X11/XWayland is also available in the same session, but does not reproduce the issue.

The issue is not caused by a tray event being delivered on the wrong thread:
- The callback runs on Avalonia's UI thread.
- Show(), Activate(), and BringIntoView() complete without an exception.
- The problem remains with normal and floating window states.

Relevant native Wayland implementation path:
- Hide() disposes the current Wayland sink/surface.
- Show() recreates it through `CurrentSink = new Sink(this, true)`.
- The source contains a TODO to re-apply window state during this recreation:
  https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia/blob/11564814b32a844145ed1450b5ea42e53889bf3a/src/Avalonia.Wayland/WindowImpl.cs#L183

title: undefined symbol: __wasm_longjmp (referenced by root reference (e.g. compiled C/C++ code)) after added a reference
author: JosepBalague
body:
### Describe the bug

undefined symbol: __wasm_longjmp (referenced by root reference (e.g. compiled C/C++ code)) when adding a reference

[AvaloniaClassLibrary.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29986121/AvaloniaClassLibrary.zip)

### To Reproduce

1. Set as startup poject "CrossApp.Browser"
2. Clear and rebuild solution. No errors found.
3. Remove comments of PDFtoImage in avalonia.dm.csproj
4. Clear and rebuild solution. 4 Errors found:

The command "emcc "@C:\Program Files\dotnet\packs\Microsoft.NETCore.App.Runtime.Mono.browser-wasm\10.0.9\runtimes\browser-wasm\native\src\emcc-default.rsp" -fexceptions "@C:\Program Files\dotnet\packs\Microsoft.NETCore.App.Runtime.Mono.browser-wasm\10.0.9\runtimes\browser-wasm\native\src\emcc-link.rsp" "@D:\SRC\Protos\AvaloniaClassLibrary\AvaloniaClassLibrary\CrossApp\CrossApp.Browser\obj\Debug\net10.0-browser\wasm\for-build\emcc-link.rsp"" exited with code 1.

'"C:\Program Files\dotnet\packs\Microsoft.NET.Runtime.Emscripten.3.1.56.Node.win-x64\10.0.9\tools\bin\node.exe" "C:\Program Files\dotnet\packs\Microsoft.NET.Runtime.Emscripten.3.1.56.Sdk.win-x64\10.0.9\tools\emscripten\src\compiler.mjs" c:\temp\tmpboc43wet.json' failed (returned 1)

undefined symbol: __wasm_longjmp (referenced by root reference (e.g. compiled C/C++ code))

Aborting compilation due to previous errors



### Expected behavior

Normal execution with PDFtoImage reference added

### Avalonia version

12.1.0

### OS

Windows

### Additional context

Using browse project. The reference works fine in desktop project

Using Waterfox 6.6.16.1

title: Expose text contrast/gamma control on TextOptions (light-on-dark text renders too heavy; no web-like font-smoothing knob)
author: jpgarza93
body:
### Summary

Avalonia (Skia) renders antialiased text noticeably **heavier when it is light-on-dark than dark-on-light**, and there is currently no API to correct it. Browsers expose this via `-webkit-font-smoothing`/`font-smooth` plus an internal text **gamma/contrast**; WPF exposes `RenderOptions.ClearTypeHint` / `TextOptions`. Avalonia exposes neither an effective equivalent on macOS. Please consider adding a **text contrast/gamma (or "font smoothing") control** to `TextOptions` - Skia already supports text gamma/contrast internally via the scaler context.

### Motivation / problem

The root cause is gamma-incorrect alpha blending of glyph-edge coverage: on a dark background the partial-coverage AA pixels blend brighter than linearly correct, so stems bloom and text looks bolder than the same glyphs on a light background. This is the classic "dark mode text looks bold" effect, and it is measurable, not just perceptual.

### Evidence (measured)

Rendered identical text (IBM Plex Sans, macOS, Skia, real headless frame capture), then summed ink coverage (Σ |pixel - background| over the glyph pixels):

| Font size | dark (light-on-dark) | light (dark-on-light) | delta |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 40px | 3,840,957 | 3,524,561 | **+9%** |
| 13px (typical UI) | 613,592 | 471,329 | **+30%** |

The effect is worst at real UI sizes.

### What doesn't work today (verified no-ops on macOS/Skia)

- **`TextOptions.TextRenderingMode`** - `SubpixelAntialias`, `Antialias`, and the default all produce **byte-identical** output on macOS (Skia never does subpixel LCD text here). No effect on perceived weight.
- **`TextOptions.TextHintingMode`** - `None`/`Light`/`Strong`/default all produce **byte-identical** output for fonts without bytecode hints (Skia autohints regardless). No effect on weight.
- **Lowering foreground contrast** - reduces the bloom slightly but does not thin strokes; it just makes text greyer/hazier and hurts legibility.
- The only workaround that actually matches light-mode weight is **shipping a separate lighter-weight font face for dark mode** (e.g. wght ≈ 360 as "Regular"), which is a heavy, per-app hack.

### Proposed solution

Add a text-gamma / text-contrast control (or a "font smoothing" enum) to `TextOptions`, so apps can neutralize the light-on-dark over-weighting globally - conceptually the same lever browsers expose. Skia supports adjusting text gamma/contrast, so this should be pluggable in the `GlyphRunImpl.CreateFont` / scaler-context path.

### Related

- #13265 - text rendering quality regression with `TextRenderingMode="Antialias"` (open)
- #9279 - "Symbol font glyphs rendered thicker in avalonia" (maintainer confirmed Skia AA is heavier than native DirectWrite)
- #12162 - request for explicit font-rendering control

### Environment

- Avalonia 12.1, Skia backend, macOS. (Believed cross-platform; most visible on macOS where no subpixel path exists.)

title: Rendering performance issue when using Skia with GPU backends
author: PopSlime
body:
### Describe the bug

When using **Skia** with **GPU backends**, for each render pass, Avalonia creates a new render target and a new surface, instead of reusing the old ones, as the code shown below.

https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia/blob/bc3001bf77329f9f8d1db14a2d952d46cfe0414c/src/Skia/Avalonia.Skia/Gpu/OpenGl/GlRenderTarget.cs#L91-L94

(*This code is for OpenGL, but the same logic can be found in the code for Metal and Vulkan as well.*)

In addition, the `GRContext` is reset every frame as well.

Is there a reason that we have to recreate these objects instead of reusing the old ones whenever possible with GPU backends?

### To Reproduce

Reproduced in every project using Skia with GPU backends, when any visual is rendered.

### Expected behavior

The render target and the surface are reused whenever possible, and recreated only when necessary.

### Avalonia version

11.3.13

### OS

Windows

### Additional context

_No response_

title: ComboBox dropdown does not scroll to the selected item when opened
author: zrt2399
body: ### Describe the bug When opening a ComboBox, the dropdown does not automatically scroll to the currently selected item. ﻿ This makes it difficult to see the current selection in long lists. ### To Reproduce Run `ControlCatalog.Desktop`. Navigate to the ComboBox page. Select an item. Close the dropdown. Open the dropdown again. ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Avalonia version 12.1.0 ### OS _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

title: Vector Path edge anti-aliasing regressed in 12.1.0 (Windows GPU)
author: jurzik
body: ### Describe the bug Stroked `Path` / `StreamGeometry` shapes (icons) render with **softer / different edge anti-aliasing on Avalonia 12.1.0** compared to **12.0.5**, on Windows with the default GPU (D3D/ANGLE) backend. **Text** rendered next to the same shapes is **byte-identical** between the two versions — only the vector geometry changes. It is subtle but consistent, and most visible at small sizes. The test shape is the standard **Lucide "info" icon** (a common open-source vector icon set) — plain stroked vector paths that should rasterize crisply at any size; this is native vector geometry, so no softening/pixelation is expected. Measured on a same-size, same-position, DPI-aware capture at 100% scaling: the reference text is **byte-identical (0 changed pixels)**, while all changed pixels fall **entirely on the icon edges**. A real app menu (identical menu, same size/position) shows the same split: **text = 0 changed px, icons = 248 changed px**. `comparison_24px.png` shows it clearest — on 12.1.0 the "i" dot goes from a solid dark square to a faint gray one and the circle edges are rougher. `repro2_heat.png` is the diff heatmap: the icon edges light up, the text line does not. ### To Reproduce Minimal project attached (`AvaloniaGeometryEdgeAA/`). On **Windows**: [AvaloniaGeometryEdgeAA.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29929574/AvaloniaGeometryEdgeAA.zip) 1. `dotnet run -c Release` on **Avalonia 12.1.0**; screenshot the window. 2. In `AvaloniaGeometryEdgeAA.csproj`, change the three `Avalonia*` `PackageReference` versions to **12.0.5**; rebuild; screenshot the window at the same position. 3. Diff the two screenshots — the reference text is identical; the icon edges differ. Note: **headless / software rendering shows no difference** — only the GPU-composited path regresses. ### Expected behavior Vector-geometry edge anti-aliasing is stable and crisp, as on 12.0.5. Native vector rendering should not soften across a minor version update. ### Avalonia version 12.1.0 ### OS Windows ### Additional context Suspected cause (leads, unverified): - **#21682** — "Composition-aware geometries and drawing change detection" (geometry became a compositor-serialized resource, `ServerCompositionSimpleGeometry`). - The **SkiaSharp bump to 3.119.4** in 12.1 (path-edge rasterization). Only geometry is affected; text (a separate glyph path) is not — which points at the geometry serialization/rasterization rather than a general AA change. Attached images: `comparison_24px.png` (24px, clearest), `comparison_96px.png` (96px, effect is proportionally tiny but present), `repro2_heat.png` (repro diff heatmap), `actions-menu-1205.png` / `actions-menu-1210.png` (real app menu, 12.0.5 vs 12.1.0) and `realapp_menu_heat.png` (real-app diff: text 0 px, icons 248 px).

title: Focus stays on detached menu item after click
author: seleborg
body: ### Describe the bug I was working on my own app when I hit this: If I used a menu item to delete an item in a list, Ctrl+Z to undo (from a menu item) wouldn't work afterwards until I clicked another list item. Since my code never does anything to put the focus somewhere useful, I would expect the keyboard shortcut to go to the main window, which handles menu commands. But nothing happened. I asked Claude Opus 4.8 to analyse the root cause, and it came to the conclusion that the focus remained on the now-detached menu item. The framework then tries to walk up the tree to find someone who can handle the keyboard event, which leads nowhere on the detached menu item. Whether this is correct or not I'm not 100% sure, but the observed behaviour *seems* wrong: The menu item shortcut *should* work if it's not disabled, even if nothing is focused. To reproduce this, I asked Claude to create a minimal example. It has a counter that can incremented by pressing up (to have a behaviour dependent on focus) and a list with three items. There's a menu item to remove the selected list item. Before clicking that menu item, pressing up increments. After clicking it, pressing up doesn't do anything until I click back into the list. ### To Reproduce - Build and start the attached project - Press up a few times -> Number increments - Select an item in the list - Use the menu item to delete that item - Press up -> **Nothing changes** - Click into the list - Press up -> Number increments again ### Expected behavior Since the window is still the active window, I'd expect the keyboard event to be handled. ### Avalonia version 12.0.5 ### OS Windows ### Additional context Attaching sample app project and screenshots thereof. [AvaloniaMenuFocusRepro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29919964/AvaloniaMenuFocusRepro.zip)

title: Window freezes on Linux ARM64 when Height exceeds 4096 pixels ,dual monitor check
author: Mohamad-Rahimian
body:
### Describe the bug

Describe the bug
On Raspberry Pi 4 or 5 (Linux Bullseye ARM64), Avalonia hangs when a Window height exceeds approximately 4096 pixels. with dual monitor.
The application works correctly until the window height becomes larger than 4096.
The same hardware, monitor configuration and resolution work correctly with WinForms/Mono.

### To Reproduce

To Reproduce
Create a simple Avalonia application.
var window = new Window
{
    Width = 1892,
    Height = 6230
};
window.Show();

When the height is below 4096, everything works.
When the height is greater than 4096, the UI freezes. Mouse/touch input also stops responding.

Expected behavior

The window should open normally regardless of its height, provided the display resolution supports it.

Environment
Avalonia 12.0.5
.NET 10 (also testing with .NET 8)
Raspberry Pi 4 or 5 
Linux Bullseye ARM64
X11 Desktop
Custom display resolution: 1892 × 6230
Additional information

The monitor actually supports this resolution and works correctly with WinForms/Mono.
This appears to be related to Avalonia's Linux rendering backend (possibly Skia or the X11 backend) when the window height exceeds 4096 pixels.

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Avalonia version

12.0.5

### OS

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: CommandBar layout should be configureable
author: timunie
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When you create a CommandBar, all the Buttons will be on the right side. This may not be intended for all Apps. Consider to add an enum for it. 

### Describe the solution you'd like

Add following API:

```cs
Enum CommandPosition
{
    Left,
    Center,
    Right
}
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Add Style-classes 

### Additional context

_No response_

title: DataGrid: ScrollBar context menu commands don't work as expected
author: zrt2399
body: ### Describe the bug When using a `DataGrid`, several commands in the `ScrollBar` context menu do not behave as expected. The following menu items are affected: - Scroll to Top - Scroll to Bottom - Page Up - Page Down - Left Edge - Right Edge - Page Left - Page Right These commands do not scroll to the expected position. ### To Reproduce 1. Run the `Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid` sample project. 2. Right-click the `ScrollBar`. 3. Click any of the following context menu commands: * Scroll to Top * Scroll to Bottom * Page Up * Page Down * Left Edge * Right Edge * Page Left * Page Right ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Avalonia version 12.1.0 ### OS _No response_ ### Additional context https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia/pull/19413

title: UpdateTarget does not revert value when UpdateSourceTrigger is Explicit
author: grokys

title: Back navigation using TopLevel.BackRequested does not work when a SplitView in Overlay mode is involved
author: gentledepp
body:
### Describe the bug

When having a UI with a splitview and it is configured in overlay mode (e.g. mobile phone narrow screens), navigating back using the system back button (e.g. Android back button or navigate back gesture) does not work:
Nothing happens.

This is due to the fact that SplitView "steals" the event even if its Pane is not open!
PR will follow shortly!!

### To Reproduce

Open a page containing a splitview configured in overlay mode on your mobile (e.g. android phone)
Try navigating back using the back gesture or back button - done

### Expected behavior

Back navigation should work.
If the pane is open (in overlay mode) the pane should close
Otherwise the SplitView should ignore the event and not set "Handled=true"

### Avalonia version

12.0.5 and 11.x 

### OS

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: NavigationPage PageTransition causes severe GC/jank when navigating away from ControlCatalog ListBoxPage

body: ### Describe the bug Switching away from `ControlCatalog.Pages.ListBoxPage` is noticeably slow and triggers repeated GC when `NavigationPage.PageTransition` is enabled. The issue disappears if the `NavigationPage` transition is disabled in `samples/ControlCatalog/MainView.xaml`: ```xml ``` After disabling PageTransition: ### To Reproduce 1. Run `ControlCatalog`. 2. Navigate to the `ListBox` page. 3. Switch to another page. 4. Observe that the UI freezes noticeably and GC runs repeatedly. ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Avalonia version 12.0.5 ### OS _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

title: Add hotplug support for LibInputBackend via udev context
author: MikhTrush

title: iOS Deep Linking still does not work
author: PzVeCo
body:
### Describe the bug

I see the url is now being passed correctly in the `bool IAvaloniaAppInternalDelegate.OpenUrl(NSUrl url)` function from [this PR.](https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia/pull/21394) However, the `_onActivated?.Invoke(this, new ProtocolActivatedEventArgs(uri));` does not properly handle the url. 

The app is opened via link but the `ActivatableLifetime` is never triggered so the uri cannot be passed into the application.

### To Reproduce

1. Implement deep link through a custom URL scheme. 
2. Add `ActivatableLifetime` listening to your `app.axaml.cs` `OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted()` method.
3. Navigate to your custom url via browser. 
4. Breakpoint at `Avalonia.iOS.AvaloniaAppDelegate.OpenUrl` (`Line 93`) and confirm that the url is properly passed. 
5. After `_onActivated` is invoked, no `activatableLifetime` is triggered on iOS (does work on Android).

### Expected behavior

After link is clicked, `activatableLifetime` is triggered in the `app.axaml.cs` file with `Activated` status, and url handling implementation is then fired off.

### Avalonia version

12.0.5

### OS

iOS

### Additional context

I am able to get it to work on **Android** so the `activatableLifetime` implementation is correct.

In my info.plist:
```
<key>CFBundleURLTypes</key>
<array>
	<dict>
		<key>CFBundleTypeRole</key>
		<string>Editor</string>

		<key>CFBundleURLName</key>
		<string>pzCustomName</string>

		<key>CFBundleURLSchemes</key>
		<array>
			<string>pzCustomScheme</string>
		</array>
	</dict>
</array>
```

Implementation of `activatableLifetime`:

```
this.TryGetFeature<IActivatableLifetime>(out IActivatableLifetime? activatableLifetime);
Avalonia.Logging.Logger.Sink?.Log(Avalonia.Logging.LogEventLevel.Fatal, "pzCustomApp", activatableLifetime, $"#### activateableLifetime in initialize: ####\n\n\n{activatableLifetime}");

if (activatableLifetime is not null)
{
    activatableLifetime.Activated += (s, a) =>
    {                
        Console.WriteLine("ActivatableLifetime activated.");
        if (a is ProtocolActivatedEventArgs protocolArgs && protocolArgs.Kind == ActivationKind.OpenUri)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("ActivatableLifetime has fired.");
            DeepLinkNav(protocolArgs.Uri);
        }
    };
}
```

title: Android accessibility: plain MenuItem (ToggleType=None) crashes with "Peer instance does not implement T"
author: mikegoatly
body:
### Describe the bug

On Android, opening a `MenuFlyout` containing plain `MenuItems` crashes the app with `System.InvalidOperationException: Peer instance does not implement T.` as soon as an accessibility service reads the menu's node (e.g. TalkBack, an OEM screen reader, or any app that registers an accessibility service).

```
FATAL EXCEPTION: main
android.runtime.JavaProxyThrowable: [System.InvalidOperationException]: Peer instance does not implement T.
  at Avalonia.Android.Automation.NodeInfoProvider`1[[Avalonia.Automation.Provider.IToggleProvider, ...]].GetProvider
  at Avalonia.Android.Automation.ToggleNodeInfoProvider.PopulateNodeInfo
  at Avalonia.Android.AvaloniaAccessHelper.OnPopulateNodeForVirtualView
  at AndroidX.CustomView.Widget.ExploreByTouchHelper.n_OnPopulateNodeForVirtualView_...
```

It only happens with an accessibility service running - without one, the menu works fine.



### To Reproduce

Minimal repro project attached (single Button + MenuFlyout with two plain MenuItems). 
[AvaloniaMenuItemA11yCrash.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29763085/AvaloniaMenuItemA11yCrash.zip)

1. Deploy to any Android device/emulator.
2. Tap the button to open the MenuFlyout.
3. Trigger an accessibility tree walk — either enable TalkBack and touch the screen, or from a host shell `run adb shell uiautomator dump` (enough to hit the crash; no accessibility service needs to be installed).
4. App crashes immediately.

### Expected behavior

Opening a `MenuFlyout` with plain `MenuItems` does not crash when an accessibility service reads the tree.

### Avalonia version

12.0.5

### OS

Android

### Additional context

Reproduced on an emulator (Pixel 7, API 35) via uiautomator dump; originally reported from production on a physical OnePlus (Android 12) with an accessibility service active.
